one_line_thesis: Heritage Global is a small multi-segment asset-services croupier (auctions, lab resale, NLEX charged-off brokerage) with specialty lending / JV assets and a new DebtX platform, trading below book pending earnings-power confirmation.

decision.next_action: Close critical evidence gaps before freezing a decision-grade packet.

components.label: Auction, ALT, and NLEX fee franchises (after corporate overhead)
components.ownership_claim: Auction, ALT, and NLEX fee franchises (after corporate overhead)
components.evidence: FACT FY2025 10-K segment op. income: Auction/Liquidation $2.671M, Refurbishment & Resale $1.659M, Brokerage (NLEX) $6.116M; corporate ($4.965M). CALC fee segments after corporate = $5.481M; after ~25% tax proxy ≈ $4.11M. JUDGMENT low/base/high ≈ 8×/10×/12× that after-tax run-rate ($0.95/$1.18/$1.42 before rounding). Q1 2026 still profitable but softer (NI $0.717M). Sources: 10-K FY2025 segment note; 10-Q Q1 2026.
components.scenario_assumptions: Low/base/high change earnings power and exit multiple; not a single terminal multiple on revenue.
components.tax_and_realization_adjustments: Low/base/high incorporate ownership, financing, tax, and realization adjustments in evidence.
components.falsifier: Through-cycle after-tax fee earnings fall such that capitalized value is below the low case.

components.label: Specialty lending notes receivable
components.evidence: FACT Q1 2026: current notes receivable net $4.369M + non-current $4.182M = $8.551M ($0.25/sh). Specialty Lending FY2025 op. income only $0.230M. JUDGMENT haircut carrying for credit/collection risk.
components.scenario_assumptions: Low/base/high vary recovery vs carrying on the notes book.
components.tax_and_realization_adjustments: Low/base/high incorporate ownership, financing, tax, and realization adjustments in evidence.
components.falsifier: Primary evidence supports economics below the low case or reveals an omitted claim.

components.label: Equity-method investments (JVs / participating interests)
components.evidence: FACT Q1 2026 carrying value $19.442M ($0.56/sh). FY2025 equity-method earnings only $0.123M (vs $2.688M in 2024). JUDGMENT: base discounts carrying for illiquidity and lumpy realizations; high near carrying after recent DLZ exit evidence.
components.scenario_assumptions: Cases vary realizable value of JV / participating interests vs carrying.
components.tax_and_realization_adjustments: Low/base/high incorporate ownership, financing, tax, and realization adjustments in evidence.
components.falsifier: Primary evidence supports economics below the low case or reveals an omitted claim.

components.label: Cash, inventory, PP&E net of operating working liabilities
components.ownership_claim: Cash, inventory, PP&E net of operating working liabilities
components.evidence: FACT Q1: cash $11.566M + inventory $5.348M + AR $1.725M + PP&E $12.029M = $30.668M; less AP $5.174M + payables to sellers $5.317M + other current liab $0.896M = $11.387M → net ≈ $19.3M ($0.56/sh) before debt/leases. JUDGMENT haircut for inventory realization and PP&E specialized-use risk.
components.scenario_assumptions: Low/base/high vary inventory and PP&E realization; cash near face.
components.tax_and_realization_adjustments: Low/base/high incorporate ownership, financing, tax, and realization adjustments in evidence.
components.falsifier: Primary evidence supports economics below the low case or reveals an omitted claim.

components.label: Third-party debt and lease liabilities
components.evidence: FACT Q1: non-current third-party debt $4.100M; lease liabilities current $0.818M + non-current $0.619M = $1.437M; total ≈ $5.537M ($0.16/sh).
components.scenario_assumptions: Cases vary paydown pace and lease run-off.
components.tax_and_realization_adjustments: Low/base/high incorporate ownership, financing, tax, and realization adjustments in evidence.
components.falsifier: Primary evidence supports economics below the low case or reveals an omitted claim.

components.label: Series N preferred liquidation preference
components.evidence: FACT Q1: 563 Series N preferred shares with $1,000/share liquidation preference over common = $0.563M ($0.02/sh).
components.scenario_assumptions: Fixed senior claim at stated preference.
components.tax_and_realization_adjustments: Low/base/high incorporate ownership, financing, tax, and realization adjustments in evidence.
components.falsifier: Primary evidence supports economics below the low case or reveals an omitted claim.

components.label: DebtX commercial-loan brokerage platform (2026 acquisition)
components.evidence: FACT: Jan 1, 2026 acquired substantially all assets of The Debt Exchange Inc. for ~$8.5M (10-Q). Q1 Commercial Loans segment op. loss ($0.606M). Goodwill rose to $12.747M; intangibles to $6.066M. JUDGMENT: only incremental value beyond fee-franchise / tangible assets already counted; failure value zero if platform does not scale.
components.scenario_assumptions: Low failure; base modest earn-in; high assumes platform approaches NLEX-like contribution over time.

probability_and_timing.timing_basis: The estimate is assessed over the five-year valuation horizon following the January 1, 2026 acquisition, rather than as immediate owner cash.
probability_and_timing.probability_basis: The low/base/high range explicitly assigns failure, modest earn-in, and a NLEX-like contribution path; Q1 2026 Commercial Loans operating loss supports a conservative base.
probability_and_timing.remaining_capital_basis: No separate owner-funded expansion capital is modeled; the $8.5M acquisition consideration is already paid and the incremental option value is after the current operating loss and asset base.

components.tax_and_realization_adjustments: Low/base/high incorporate ownership, financing, tax, and realization adjustments in evidence.
components.falsifier: DebtX remains loss-making and impaired below the low case (zero).

committee.next_action: Freeze the evidence packet and select three method-independent raters.

gaps.question: Reconcile the ~$8.5M DebtX acquisition to incremental owner value beyond goodwill/intangibles already elsewhere.
gaps.evidence_required: Primary purchase accounting plus forward contribution evidence.
gaps.acceptance_test: The evidence reproduces the low/base/high option value, resolves overlap, and identifies a falsifier and monitoring source.
gaps.progress_note: not_met 2026-07-16: purchase price and Q1 loss known; earn-in bridge missing. Economic-validation treatment added 2026-07-17 for probability, timing, and remaining capital; the forward contribution evidence requirement remains unmet.

gaps.question: Reconcile notes and equity-method carrying values to expected realizations and credit risk.
gaps.evidence_required: Primary footnotes plus independently reproducible recovery schedule.
gaps.acceptance_test: The evidence reproduces the low/base/high bridge, resolves overlap, and identifies a falsifier and monitoring source.
gaps.progress_note: partially_met 2026-07-16: carrying values sourced; asset-level schedules incomplete.

gaps.question: Reconcile through-cycle fee earnings after corporate costs and cash tax to the low/base/high capitalized values.
gaps.evidence_required: Primary segment history plus reproducible after-tax earnings bridge and multiple rationale.
gaps.acceptance_test: The evidence reproduces the low/base/high bridge, resolves overlap with asset components, and identifies a falsifier and monitoring source.
gaps.progress_note: partially_met 2026-07-16: FY2025 segment op. income rebuilt; multiples/tax still provisional.

method_fit.label: High-return compounder
applicability_tests: Owner earnings can be separated from stock compensation, working capital, and maintenance capital.
Incremental returns on retained capital are observable and durable.
The competitive advantage explains pricing, retention, share, or cost outcomes.
Terminal value is supported by a reinvestment runway rather than an unexplained multiple.
failure_modes: growth projected without its capital cost
terminal value unsupported by a durable mechanism

validation_cohort.purpose: Test depletion, commodity exposure, finite reserves, and royalty-unit reconciliation.
Test tangible capital, credit losses, funding, regulation, and excess-capital realization.
Test owner earnings, capital-light reinvestment, cyclicality, and buyback value creation.
Test normalized margins, industry supply response, replacement cost, and reinvestment timing.
Test product concentration, pipeline probabilities, patent cliffs, and catalyst timing.

method_fit.rule: Use one primary method and no more than two corroborating methods; irrelevant methods and personas remain silent.

outcomes.weighting_rule: Do not change a persona's relevance within a power zone until at least 20 attributable, dividend-aware outcomes exist there; never reweight automatically.

attribution.explanation: A complete security value is intentionally withheld while material components are unpriced. Attribution restarts after the first proof-complete baseline.

component_valuation.decision_rule: Use the complete low/base/high component schedule. Embedded components are estimated but not added twice.
